issues.title: Shipped card keeps a live "running" badge from its own lingering gogo-done-<slug> session (acceptance signal not literally met)
issues.description: The plan's Goal / acceptance signal is "right after a real /gogo:done, tmux list-sessions shows no live gogo-* session for the shipped slug, and the board badge is truthful." But /gogo:done runs inside a board-launched, INTERACTIVE claude session named gogo-done-<slug> (Launch -> TmuxNewSessionArgs, no -p; move.go d/m). The FR3 self-guard deliberately spares that hosting session (verified: Sweep() skips sess==Self before shouldReap, sweep.go:54). Interactive claude does NOT exit when /gogo:done returns; it idles at its prompt, so gogo-done-<slug> stays a LIVE gogo-* session for the shipped slug. The board badge for that (now shipped) card is computed by badge() -> hasLiveSession -> liveSessionFor -> launch.SessionMatchesSlug, and SessionMatchesSlug("gogo-done-<slug>","<slug>") is true (ActionDone is in the match set, launch.go:339). renderCard (view.go) calls badge() for every card incl. the changelog column, so the shipped card renders "running" until the user quits that claude (then FR4 closes the pane) or a later out-of-band gogo sweep reaps it (that feature is terminal). So the DRIVING gogo-go-<slug> reap (the headline win) IS delivered, but the acceptance signal's "no live gogo-* session for the shipped slug" is not literally met for the hosting done session, and the badge can still read "running" on a shipped card for a window. This is inherent to self-reaping (you cannot reap the session you run in) - not a code defect - but the plan's wording overstates what is achievable.
issues.proposed_solution: NEEDS-USER-DECISION. The implementation of the accepted approach (D1=A plain sweep + FR3 self-guard, now D4=B targeted) is correct; the gap is inherent. Options for the user: (a) accept and soften the plan/acceptance-signal wording to "no live DRIVING (gogo-go/gogo-plan) session for the shipped slug; the hosting gogo-done session self-corrects on quit (FR4) or next out-of-band sweep"; or (b) as a follow-up feature, suppress the "running" badge on a shipped/terminal card whose only live session is its own gogo-done-<slug> host (a board-side, badge-only change - out of this feature's Go/skill scope). Do not attempt a code fix here that self-reaps the host - that reintroduces the truncation the self-guard exists to prevent.
issues.fix_summary: ACCEPTED as works-as-designed (user, D4->B, 2026-07-12); round-2 review re-confirms the disposition is sound. The ship's own gogo-done-<slug> host cannot be self-reaped without truncating the ship (that is exactly what the FR3 self-guard prevents); it closes when the user quits it (FR4) or on a later sweep. There is genuinely no safe code fix inside this feature's scope. Honestly documented as a known cosmetic limitation in docs/cli-contract.md (0.17.0 self-guard note, lines 205-206) + adjustments.md - NOT silently dropped. No code change.

issues.title: A ship's best-effort sweep can truncate a DIFFERENT feature's concurrent /gogo:done (self-guard only protects the sweep's own host)
issues.description: The FR3 self-guard is exact-name only: Sweep() skips just sess==sw.Self (the current gogo-done-<slug>). At round 1 a plain `gogo sweep` still scanned ALL live gogo-* sessions and reaped every one whose owning feature is terminal, so feature x's ship-sweep could Kill a concurrent gogo-done-z (z already flipped `shipped` in step 5, still finishing steps 6-7), SIGKILLing z's /gogo:done mid-flight. The one-owner lock is per-feature, so two features' done sessions CAN run concurrently.
issues.proposed_solution: NEEDS-USER-DECISION (narrow trigger, low impact). Recorded fixes were (a) spare all live gogo-done-* sessions, or (b) adopt the recorded D1=B targeted `gogo sweep <slug...>`. Resolved as (b) via D4->B.
issues.fix_summary: FIXED via D4->B (targeted ship-reap) in round 2, and VERIFIED by round-2 re-review. Added Sweeper.Only []string + matchesOnly()/inScope() (sweep.go:32,63-69,96-125): when Only is non-empty the session scan AND the lock/registry cleanup are restricted to the named slug(s). cmdSweep now parses `gogo sweep [<slug>...]` (each slug validSlug-guarded, else exit 1) and the /gogo:done reap step now runs targeted `gogo sweep <member-slug>...`. VERIFICATION (traced against the real tree): for feature x shipping (Self=gogo-done-x, Only=[x]) with a concurrent terminal z whose gogo-done-z is live - (1) matchesOnly("gogo-done-z",["x"]) uses the SAME exact launch.SessionMatchesSlug parse (no substring; z != any gogo-<action>-x base nor a gogo-go-x-<digits> collision form) => false => the session-scan `continue`s and gogo-done-z is never Kill'd; (2) cleanupTerminalRegistries/cleanupStaleLocks skip z because inScope("z")==false, so z's registry/lock are untouched. Whole-board mode is byte-identical to round 1: matchesOnly/inScope both short-circuit len(Only)==0 => true, so the added `continue`/guards are never taken. TestSweepTargetedOnlyNamedSlug asserts gogo-done-z + orphan + self are all spared while gogo-go-x is reaped; TestSweepSparesSelf + TestSweepReapsOrphansAndTerminal + TestSweepDryRunKillsNothing still pass; gofmt/vet clean, go test green (re-run locally).

issues.title: gogo sweep command-surface enumerations not synced with the new [<slug>...] targeted arg (README.md + gogo-cli SKILL still show `gogo sweep [--dry-run]`)
issues.description: The D4=B delta added a positional [<slug>...] argument to `gogo sweep` (cmdSweep now parses it - go.go:222-242; sweepHelp - go.go:59-76; and docs/cli-contract.md:52 + the 0.17.0 block were all updated to `gogo sweep [--dry-run] [<slug>...]`). But two OTHER curated command-surface enumerations that DO list positional args per command were left showing the pre-arg form `gogo sweep [--dry-run]`: (1) README.md:405 - the 'Scriptable' one-liner list, which elsewhere spells out args e.g. `gogo go [<slug>] [--attach] [--takeover]`, `gogo view <target> [...]`; and (2) skills/gogo-cli/SKILL.md:50 - the CLI command table, which shows `gogo go [<slug>]`, `gogo plan <slug>`, `gogo view <target>`, `gogo trash [restore <entry>]`. Both now under-document the new arg, so a reader of either sees `gogo sweep` as taking no slug. This trips coding-rules.md 'Keep enumerations in sync' (README.md is named explicitly) and code-review-standards.md item #1 ('every enumeration that changed is in sync across ... README.md; no place still describes the old behaviour. Grep the old terms.'). Note this is a genuine ROUND-2 regression: round-1 review-01.md (lines 35-37) recorded the README sweep enumeration as accurate - it WAS at round 1 because the arg did not yet exist; the D4=B delta is what made it stale. Verified against the tree: `grep -rn 'gogo sweep \['` finds cli-contract.md:52 updated but README.md:405 + gogo-cli/SKILL.md:50 stale. Explicitly NOT part of this finding: cli/main.go printHelp (line 76 + the 'gogo sweep flags:' block at 90-91) intentionally shows only a description + `--dry-run` and defers full usage to `gogo sweep --help` (sweepHelp, which is complete + correct) - the same way its `gogo go / gogo plan flags:` block does not restate `[<slug>]` - so it is internally consistent, not stale.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. One-line edit in each stale enumerator to match cli-contract.md:52 / sweepHelp: README.md:405 -> `gogo sweep [--dry-run] [<slug>...]`; skills/gogo-cli/SKILL.md:50 table cell -> `gogo sweep [--dry-run] [<slug>...]` (optionally add one clause: 'a slug targets the reap to that feature's sessions - what /gogo:done runs at ship'). Purely additive doc sync; no code/behaviour change. (skills/gogo-cli/SKILL.md is the cockpit-cards feature's new skill in the same working tree, but it enumerates the command surface this feature changed, so syncing it is part of this surface change.) Additive, non-blocking - the authoritative `docs/cli-contract.md` table + `gogo sweep --help` are already correct, so no reader is led into an error, only an incomplete usage string.
issues.fix_summary: FIXED (doc enumeration sync). Appended `[<slug>...]` to the two stale command-surface enumerators: README.md:405 (Scriptable one-liner) -> `gogo sweep [--dry-run] [<slug>...]`; skills/gogo-cli/SKILL.md:50 (CLI table) -> `gogo sweep [--dry-run] [<slug>...]` + a clause noting a slug targets the reap (what /gogo:done runs at ship). Purely additive doc sync, no code/behaviour change. Self-verified per code-review-standards item #1 ('grep the old terms'): `grep -rn 'gogo sweep \[--dry-run\]'` now finds NO curated reader-facing enumeration on the pre-arg form - only a different feature's plan snapshot + this review's own review-02.md (both non-live artifacts). cli/main.go printHelp intentionally shows description + `--dry-run` and defers full usage to `gogo sweep --help` (correct, per the finding). A fresh review round was not spawned for a two-string doc append the reviewer specified exactly and whose verification IS the grep - within the ~3-round bound.
